_metadata.description: PatentOps Platform Control Inventory — patent prosecution workflow governance. Controls are verified via event store completeness checks and hash chain verification, not statistical sampling.

controls.control_statement: User authentication requires valid JWT token with tenant context. All API requests are authenticated and authorized against RBAC role matrix.
controls.purpose: Prevent unauthorized access to patent case data.
controls.evidence_source: Event store query: all AUTH_FAILED events
controls.verification_method: Automated event store query — zero AUTH_FAILED events with subsequent successful resource access
controls.failure_criteria: Request processed without valid JWT or with incorrect tenant scope

controls.control_statement: Role-based access control enforces that only authorized roles can perform state transitions. Only reviewer or partner roles can mark artifacts as FINAL. Only partner can override conflict checks.
controls.purpose: Enforce liability boundaries — ensure licensed professionals gate all critical decisions.
controls.evidence_source: Event store query: all state transition events, verify actor_role against allowed_roles in state machine definition
controls.verification_method: Exhaustive event scan — every CASE_STATUS_CHANGED and DOCUMENT_FINALIZED event checked against role matrix
controls.failure_criteria: State transition to FINAL/FILED performed by unauthorized role

controls.control_statement: Multi-tenant data isolation enforced via PostgreSQL Row-Level Security. All queries scoped by tenant_id. No cross-tenant JOINs permitted.
controls.purpose: Prevent cross-tenant data access which would breach client confidentiality and attorney-client privilege.
controls.evidence_source: Automated cross-tenant penetration test suite; RLS policy diff against baseline
controls.verification_method: Automated test suite — attempt cross-tenant queries from each role, all must fail
controls.failure_criteria: Any query returns data belonging to a different tenant_id

controls.control_statement: All events are append-only with SHA-256 hash chain per case. No UPDATE or DELETE operations on event store tables.
controls.purpose: Ensure prosecution record integrity and tamper detection.
controls.evidence_source: Hash chain verification job; PostgreSQL permission audit showing no UPDATE/DELETE grants on event tables
controls.verification_method: Full hash chain recomputation per case — every event's new_hash verified against recomputed value
controls.failure_criteria: Hash chain break detected, or UPDATE/DELETE permission found on event store tables

controls.control_statement: Filed documents have SHA-256 content hash recorded in event ledger at time of filing. Documents are stored as immutable versioned files.
controls.purpose: Enable verification that filed documents match what was approved and have not been altered post-filing.
controls.verification_method: Recompute SHA-256 of stored file and compare against DOCUMENT_FILED event content_hash
controls.failure_criteria: Content hash mismatch between stored file and ledger record

controls.control_statement: AI-generated content is always watermarked as DRAFT and requires human review by a licensed professional (reviewer/partner) before any state transition to FINAL or FILED. AI sidecar never writes directly to the evidence ledger.
controls.purpose: Prevent AI hallucinations from reaching filed patent documents. Maintain professional liability boundaries.
controls.evidence_source: Event store: for every AI_DRAFT_CREATED event, verify existence of subsequent AI_DRAFT_ACCEPTED with actor_role in [reviewer, partner] before DOCUMENT_FINALIZED
controls.verification_method: Event chain completeness — every AI_DRAFT_CREATED must have a corresponding human review event before FILED
controls.failure_criteria: AI draft reaching FILED status without intervening human review event by licensed professional

controls.control_statement: All claim amendments preserve full diff history including previous text, new text, amendment reason, and version lineage. No amendment can be made without creating a CLAIM_AMENDED event.
controls.purpose: Maintain complete prosecution history for estoppel analysis. Claim amendments made during prosecution can limit patent scope — full lineage is legally required.
controls.evidence_source: Per-case event chain: all CLAIM_AMENDED events for case
controls.verification_method: Version continuity check — no claim version gaps (version N+1 must follow version N with corresponding event)
controls.failure_criteria: Claim version gap detected, or amendment event missing previous_text/new_text/diff

controls.control_statement: Conflict of interest check is automatically initiated at case INTAKE. Case cannot transition to DRAFTING without a completed CONFLICT_CHECK_COMPLETED event. CONFLICT_FOUND requires partner-level override with documented justification.
controls.purpose: Prevent professional ethics violations from accepting conflicting client representations.
controls.evidence_source: Event store: for every CASE_CREATED, verify CONFLICT_CHECK_COMPLETED exists before CASE_STATUS_CHANGED to DRAFTING
controls.verification_method: Event chain completeness — every case must have conflict check between INTAKE and DRAFTING
controls.failure_criteria: Case transitioned to DRAFTING without CONFLICT_CHECK_COMPLETED, or CONFLICT_OVERRIDE_APPROVED by non-partner role

controls.control_statement: Applicant's duty of candor is tracked per case. All known prior art references must be recorded. IDS (Information Disclosure Statement) filing status is tracked with deadline enforcement. System warns when cited references exist but no IDS has been filed.
controls.purpose: Prevent inequitable conduct findings that could render patents unenforceable. Under USPTO Rule 1.56, applicants must disclose material prior art.
controls.evidence_source: Per-case: compare known prior art references against IDS filing events
controls.verification_method: Per-case completeness — all PRIOR_ART_REFERENCE_ADDED events must have corresponding IDS coverage before case reaches CLOSED(granted)
controls.failure_criteria: Case granted with known prior art references not covered by any IDS filing

controls.control_statement: Patent family relationships (continuations, divisionals, CIPs) are explicitly tracked with bidirectional references. Priority claims are recorded with supporting evidence.
controls.purpose: Prevent inconsistent claims across related applications and ensure priority chain integrity.
controls.evidence_source: Event store: PATENT_FAMILY_LINKED events with bidirectional case_id references
controls.verification_method: Bidirectional consistency — if case A links to case B, case B must link back to case A
controls.failure_criteria: Unidirectional family link, or priority date claim without supporting filing evidence

controls.control_statement: Deadline Engine calculates statutory deadlines from jurisdiction-specific rules, performs sweep checks at configured intervals, and sends escalation notifications at defined thresholds (30, 14, 7, 3, 1 days). Deadline calculation rules are traceable to statutory/regulatory sources.
controls.purpose: Prevent missed patent deadlines which can result in irrecoverable loss of patent rights.
controls.evidence_source: Event store: complete DEADLINE lifecycle events per case; worker heartbeat logs
controls.verification_method: For every active deadline: verify warning events exist at each passed threshold; verify worker uptime via heartbeat
controls.failure_criteria: Deadline missed without prior warning events, or worker downtime exceeding check interval

controls.control_statement: Patent maintenance fees and prosecution fees are tracked with deadline enforcement. Fee payment deadlines trigger the same escalation chain as prosecution deadlines.
controls.purpose: Prevent unintentional patent abandonment due to missed fee payments.
controls.evidence_source: Event store: FEE_DEADLINE_CREATED and FEE_PAYMENT_RECORDED events per case
controls.verification_method: For every FEE_DEADLINE_CREATED, verify either FEE_PAYMENT_RECORDED before due date or active escalation chain
controls.failure_criteria: Fee deadline passed without payment event or active escalation

controls.control_statement: Missed deadlines automatically generate incident records with full audit trail and stakeholder notification. Incident records include root cause analysis fields.
controls.purpose: Ensure prompt response to missed deadlines and documented incident management.
controls.evidence_source: Event store: every DEADLINE_MISSED event must have corresponding INCIDENT_CREATED event
controls.verification_method: Event chain completeness — every DEADLINE_MISSED has a corresponding INCIDENT_CREATED within defined SLA
controls.failure_criteria: DEADLINE_MISSED event without corresponding incident record
